content.problemStatement:
–°hef has assembled a football team! Now he needs to choose a name for his team. There are NNN words in English that Chef considers funny. These words are s1,s2,‚Ä¶,sNs_1, s_2, \dots, s_Ns1‚Äã,s2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,sN‚Äã.

Chef thinks that a good team name should consist of two words such that they are not funny, but if we swap the first letters in them, the resulting words are funny. Note that under the given constraints, this means that a word is a non-empty string containing only lowercase English letters.

Can you tell Chef how many good team names there are?
constraints: s1,s2,‚Ä¶,sNs_1, s_2, \ldots, s_Ns1‚Äã,s2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,sN‚Äã contain only lowercase English letters
s1,s2,‚Ä¶,sNs_1, s_2, \ldots, s_Ns1‚Äã,s2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,sN‚Äã are pairwise distinct
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1042 \cdot 10^42‚ãÖ104
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (20 points):** s1,s2,‚Ä¶,sNs_1, s_2, \ldots, s_Ns1‚Äã,s2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,sN‚Äã contain only letters 'a' and 'b'
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated strings s1,s2,‚Ä¶,sNs_1, s_2, \ldots, s_Ns1‚Äã,s2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,sN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of ways to choose a good team name.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
2
suf mas
3
good game guys
4
hell bell best test
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The good team names are ("muf", "sas") and ("sas", "muf").
Example case 2: There are no good team names because all funny words start with 'g'.
Example case 3: The good team names are ("hest", "tell") and ("tell", "hest").

hidden.input:
3
2
suf mas
3
good game guys
4
hell bell best test
